This reference, the 18038 , dates to 1978 and is crafted in 18k solid yellow gold with the classic “champagne” dial . Featuring a sapphire crystal and the 3055 “single quick” calibre, this Day-Date is the perfect balance of vintage charm and everyday practicality . This watch is in very good condition , and is accompanied by its inner & outer box . #29 - The 18k Solid Gold Rolex Day Date 18038 w/ Box 1978 The History— The Example The Rolex Day-Date is one of the most iconic watches ever produced and has sat at the very top of the Rolex catalogue since its introduction in 1956. Crafted exclusively in precious metals and instantly recognisable by its day display at 12 o’clock, the Day-Date has long been associated with presidents, business leaders and influential figures across generations, earning it the enduring nickname of the “President.” This reference, the 18038 , dates to 1978 and represents one of the most desirable eras of Day-Date production. Crafted in 18k solid yellow gold and featuring the classic champagne dial , it captures everything collectors love about vintage Rolex. The 18038 was also an important step forward for the model, introducing a sapphire crystal and the Rolex calibre 3055 movement. Featuring the desirable single quickset date function, the calibre 3055 brought a level of practicality and convenience that earlier Day-Dates lacked, while maintaining the robustness and reliability for which Rolex is renowned. On the wrist, the watch delivers exactly what a Day-Date should: substantial without being excessive, elegant without feeling formal. The combination of yellow gold, fluted bezel and champagne dial remains one of the most timeless configurations Rolex has ever produced. This example is presented on an attractive brown leather strap, and is in very good condition , accompanied by its inner and outer box.
